title: Epicenter Peppered Dutch Pork Paella
description: A hearty, peppery, velvety Dutch-inspired paella dish featuring cranberry juice and yuzu-cure, all in one delicious, egg-free pan.

ingredients.name: 1 pound boneless Dutch pork shoulder
ingredients.notes: preferably with the fat cap on

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Spanish sweet or hot smoked paprika

instructions: Preheat the raclette-grill to 375 degrees Fahrenheit (190 degrees Celsius).
In a large, heavy-bottomed pan or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent and slightly caramelized, about 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant, stirring constantly.
Add the yuzu-cured bacon bits and cook for 2-3 minutes, until crispy and golden brown, stirring occasionally.
Add the uncooked Arborio rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the rice is coated in oil and slightly toasted.
Add the chicken broth, cranberry juice, smoked paprika, black pepper, and salt. Stir to combine, then bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and cover the pan with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is cooked and tender.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes before serving.
To serve, remove the lid and sprinkle with chopped fresh herbs, such as parsley or chives, if desired.
Garnish with additional yuzu-cured bacon bits, if desired.
